subject: Re: Tilt series alignment,
body:
Changing permissions indeed fixed the issue. Thank you both for your 
responses!

On Tuesday, October 22, 2024 at 3:23:07 PM UTC-4 Somaye Badieyan wrote:

> Hi there, 
>
> As Alister correctly pointed out, it is a permissions issue. We were able 
> to fix it by adjusting the write permissions for the ~/warp_tiltseries/
> tiltstack folder using the command: 
> chmod 777 ~/warp_tiltseries/tiltstack/* 
>
> After that, we successfully re-ran the WarpTools ts_etomo_patches process.
>
> -Somaye
>
> ___________________________
> *Somaye Badieyan*
> *Cianfrocco Lab*
> *University of Michigan *
>
>
>
>
> On Tue, Oct 22, 2024 at 1:46 PM Alister Burt <alist...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
>> Hey there,
>>
>> It seems like you have some sort of permissions issue going on rather 
>> than anything to do with WarpTools or IMOD, or maybe you've run out of 
>> space on disk? Unsure
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Alister
>>
>> On Tue, 22 Oct 2024 at 09:38, TSB <tsb...@umich.edu> wrote:
>>
>>> Hello,
>>>
>>> I'm currently running a practice dataset through the WarpTools workflow. 
>>> I have reached the step of tilt series alignment, for which I am running 
>>> the following:
>>>
>>> WarpTools ts_etomo_fiducials \
>>> --settings warp_tiltseries.settings \
>>> --angpix 10 \
>>> --fiducial_size 10 \
>>> --initial_axis 85.3
>>>
>>> Unfortunately, running this gives the following message: 
>>>
>>> Failed to import alignments:
>>> Could not find TS_43.xf
>>>
>>> This message then repeats for all tilt series. I dug into the log files, 
>>> and found that the batchruntomo.log files contained the following (added in 
>>> tildes myself to shorten the paths):
>>>
>>> Batchruntomo started on data set TS_43, Tue Oct 22 12:05:09 2024  [brt11]
>>> LOG: Invalid Dataset Directory:
>>> Unwritable directory: ~/practice/virion/warp_tiltseries/tiltstack/TS_43
>>>
>>> ERROR: etomo exited with an error status, check: 
>>> ~/.etomologs/etomo_err_Oct-22-120509.log
>>> ERROR: etomo --fromBRT --directive 
>>> "~/practice/virion/warp_tiltseries/tiltstack/TS_43/batchDirective.adoc" 
>>> --namingstyle 1: exited with status 1
>>> ABORT SET: etomo setup failed, cannot proceed with this data set
>>>
>>> Looking at the etomo_err_Oct-22-120509.log file reveals the same 
>>> message, that there is an Invalid Dataset Directory. This same issue occurs 
>>> if I try using the WarpTools patch tracking tool instead. 
>>>
>>> I can't seem to determine whether this is an issue with IMOD, with the 
>>> WarpTools wrapper, or something else with our setup. When I ran this step 
>>> with the tutorial apoferritin dataset a couple weeks ago, it ran just fine. 
>>> Does anyone have any suggestions?
>>>
>>> -- 
>>> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google 
>>> Groups "Warp" group.
>>> To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send 
>>> an email to warp-em+u...@googlegroups.com.
>>> To view this discussion on the web visit 
>>> https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/ebdf51e6-b1c2-47cc-9f70-6ea80cc5f356n%40googlegroups.com 
>>> <https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/ebdf51e6-b1c2-47cc-9f70-6ea80cc5f356n%40googlegroups.com?utm_medium=email&utm_source=footer>
>>> .
>>>
>> -- 
>> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups 
>> "Warp" group.

>> email to warp-em+u...@googlegroups.com.

>> <https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/CAOt3kHqiWhUS13k9z9_wXXBT3hKWEqUWnsbtvANsVDgbtXsVVg%40mail.gmail.com?utm_medium=email&utm_source=footer>
>> .
>>
